ext_description.message: Fork of Video Speed Controller: keyboard shortcuts for HTML5 video/audio speed, plus customizable on-screen controller styling, themed popup/options, and locales (en, ja, zh_CN).

prefs_audio_label.message:
Audio support
Show speed controls on audio elements.

prefs_remember_label.message:
Remember playback speed
Store and auto-apply the last used speed to all new videos.

prefs_start_hidden_label.message:
Hide controller by default
Controller stays hidden until toggled via shortcut.

prefs_exclusive_label.message:
Exclusive keyboard shortcuts
When possible, prevent websites from also handling VSC shortcut keys.

appearance_color_scheme_label.message:
Color theme
Use system setting, force light UI, or force dark UI.

appearance_accent_label.message:
Accent color
Applied to popup, options, highlights, and focus rings.

controller_opacity_label.message:
Controller opacity
0 = fully transparent, 1 = fully opaque. Default: 0.3

controller_button_size_label.message:
Controller button size
Font size in pixels for the speed display and buttons. Default: 14

preview_heading_label.message:
Approximate preview
Rough mock on a checker pattern (transparency). Updates live from the sliders above; empty saved values use defaults (0.3 / 14px).

speed_text_color_label.message:
Speed indicator text color
Color of the playback-rate number on the in-page controller. Hex or CSS color; leave empty for default white. Use the spectrum for quick hex picks.

panel_bg_label.message:
Controller background
Background color for the whole on-page control bar: the speed number and the control buttons (rewind, slower / faster, advance). The speed area is transparent so this color shows through behind the digits. Empty = default black. Use the text field or spectrum for hex.

controller_font_label.message:
Controller font family
Font stack for the whole on-page controller (speed + buttons). Pick a preset below (Aa preview) or type a custom comma-separated stack. Use font family names as installed on your PC; the browser picks the first available face. Leave empty for the built-in defaults.

font_tuning_desc.message: Sliders apply to the whole controller in the page. Uses CSS font-weight (100 - 900) and font-stretch as a percentage (50% - 200%). Leave defaults (Reset) to keep the extension's built-in look. Imported settings may still use keywords (e.g. bold); those show a note until you move the weight slider.

meter_weight_title.message: Stroke weight from light (top) to bold (bottom)

log_level_label.message:
Console Log Level
Set verbosity in the browser console

site_rules_label.message:
Site rules
Per-site overrides. Use a domain or regex (e.g. /\.edu$/i). Check "Disable" to turn off the extension on that site. Set a speed to override the default.

custom_css_label.message:
Custom CSS
Additional rules injected alongside the built-in defaults. Use to override positioning or add site-specific tweaks.

warn_altgr.message: This combination may conflict with AltGr input on some keyboard layouts.

warn_meta.message: Some Cmd/Meta combinations are intercepted by the OS and may not work.

err_regex_invalid.message: Error: Invalid site rule regex: "$1". Unable to save.

err_speed_range.message: Error: Speed for "$1" must be between $2 and $3.

err_css_syntax.message: Error: Controller CSS has syntax errors. Fix them before saving.

err_css_size.message: Error: Controller CSS exceeds 8KB storage limit ($1KB). Reduce CSS size.

err_save_storage.message: Error: failed to save options to storage

css_no_rules.message: No CSS rules parsed — check for syntax errors.

font_cap_weight_live_default.message: Live preview $1 — built-in default until you move & Save

font_cap_stretch_live_default.message: Live preview $1% — built-in default until you move & Save

faq_block_local.message:
The speed controls are not showing up for local videos or Incognito mode?

To enable playback of local media (e.g. File > Open File) or Incognito mode, you need to manually grant additional permissions to the extension.

  • In a new tab, navigate to chrome://extensions
  • Find "Video Speed Controller Plus" extension in the list and enable "Allow access to file URLs" and/or "Allow in Incognito".

faq_block_disable.message:
How do I disable the extension on a specific site?

Go to Advanced > Site rules and add a rule with the site's domain (e.g. example.com). Check the Disable checkbox. The extension will not inject on that site. You can also use regex patterns like /\.edu$/i to match multiple domains.

faq_block_site_speed.message:
How do I set a default speed for a specific site?

In Advanced > Site rules, add a rule with the site's domain and set the Speed field to your desired value (e.g. 2 for 2x). Leave the Disable checkbox unchecked. Every new video on that site will start at that speed.

faq_block_remember.message:
How does "Remember playback speed" interact with site rules?

When rememberSpeed is off (default), each page load starts at 1x — or the site rule speed if one matches. Your in-session speed changes apply to all new videos but are forgotten on page reload.

When rememberSpeed is on, the last speed you set is stored and persists across sessions. This stored speed takes precedence over site rule defaults. To let a site rule win again, either turn off rememberSpeed or manually reset to 1x.

faq_block_keys.message:
A site's keyboard shortcuts conflict with mine. What can I do?

Three options:

  • Add a modifier: In Settings > Shortcuts, add Shift, Ctrl, or Alt to your shortcut keys to create chord combinations the site won't intercept.
  • Rebind to unused keys: Change the default key to one the site doesn't use.
  • Enable exclusive mode: Turn on Exclusive keyboard shortcuts in Preferences to prevent sites from also handling your VSC shortcut keys.

faq_block_position.message:
The controller overlaps with site controls. How do I reposition it?

Go to Advanced > Controller CSS. Add a domain rule targeting vsc-controller using the --vsc-domain CSS variable. Example:

:root[style*='--vsc-domain: "example.com"'] vsc-controller { position: relative; top: 60px; }

You can also drag the controller to a new position on any page. See the existing rules for YouTube, Netflix, etc. as reference.

faq_block_missing.message:
The controller is not appearing on a site. Why?

Common causes:

  • The site is in your Site rules with Disable checked — remove or uncheck it.
  • The site uses Flash or a non-HTML5 player — the extension only works with native <video> and <audio> elements.
  • Hide controller by default is enabled — press your display toggle shortcut (default: V) to show it.

faq_block_spa.message:
Speed resets when I navigate on YouTube or similar single-page apps?

YouTube uses SPA navigation — the page doesn't fully reload, but the player may reinitialize. The extension restores speed from the in-session value automatically. If speed still resets, enable rememberSpeed in Preferences to persist speed across navigations and sessions.

faq_block_audio.message:
Does this work with audio elements, not just video?

Yes. When Audio support is enabled in Preferences (on by default), the controller appears on <audio> elements too. All speed controls and keyboard shortcuts work identically for audio.
